name: Access Token Abuse
notes:
This rule detects token impersonation and theft. (Example: DuplicateToken(Ex) and ImpersonateLoggedOnUser with the LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS flag.)

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Michaela Adams, Zach Mathis
SIGMA Title: Access Token Abuse
SIGMA Description: This rule tries to detect token impersonation and theft. (Example: DuplicateToken(Ex) and ImpersonateLoggedOnUser with the LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS flag.)


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5000830) Success Audit: An account was successfully logged on
when the event matches Logon Type (custom) is any of 9
when the event matches Logon Process (custom) is any of Advapi
when the event matches Authentication Package (custom) is any of Negotiate
when the event matches Impersonation Level (custom) is any of Impersonation
TD_list: APPLY Access Token Abuse on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5000830) Success Audit: An account was successfully logged on
AND when the event matches Logon Type (custom) is any of 9
AND when the event matches Logon Process (custom) is any of Advapi
AND when the event matches Authentication Package (custom) is any of Negotiate
AND when the event matches Impersonation Level (custom) is any of Impersonation
RED_list: Event description: Detects token impersonation and theft. (Example: DuplicateToken(Ex) and ImpersonateLoggedOnUser with the LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS flag.)
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
